settings.hero_intro: How to grow carnivorous plants in South Africa — core principles first, then genus-specific culture on each collection and product page.
settings.pillars_intro: Every genus bends these rules — but neglecting any one is how collections fail on the bench.
settings.climate_intro: Match your setup to temperate outdoor culture or humid tropical benches — most losses come from choosing the wrong zone.
settings.path_intro: Four steps from first genus to a stocked, stable bench.
settings.genera_text: Open a genus for trap types, culture notes, and live stock — each product page includes a cultivation dossier when data is available.
settings.cta_text: Questions about your bench or climate? Visit our contact page for advice before you order.

settings.title: Water
settings.text: Rainwater, reverse osmosis, or distilled water — avoid tap water with high TDS.

settings.title: Light
settings.text: Match intensity to genus: full sun for many Sarracenia and Dionaea; filtered light for many Nepenthes.

settings.title: Substrate
settings.text: Low-nutrient mixes: peat, sphagnum, perlite, and sand ratios depend on the genus.

settings.title: Dormancy
settings.text: Temperate species need a cool winter rest; tropical genera grow year-round with stable warmth.

settings.title: Temperate & Mediterranean zones
settings.text: Sarracenia, Dionaea, and many Drosera thrive with strong light and seasonal temperature swings. Plan a cool, bright winter for dormancy — frost-free but not warm.

settings.title: Subtropical & highland tropics
settings.text: Nepenthes, Heliamphora, and many Pinguicula want stable warmth, higher humidity, and filtered light. Highland Nepenthes need cooler nights; lowland types want heat.

settings.title: Start with the genus
settings.text: Open a genus collection below — trap type and climate zone determine light, water, and dormancy.

settings.title: Read the product dossier
settings.text: Each specimen page includes a cultivation dossier with metafield care notes when available.

settings.title: Stock your bench
settings.text: Pair plants with growing supplies — substrate, trays, and tools matched to carnivorous culture.

settings.title: Ask before you order
settings.text: Message us with photos of your setup if you are unsure about humidity, dormancy, or winter protection.

settings.question: Can I use tap water?
settings.answer: Most carnivorous plants prefer rainwater, reverse osmosis, or distilled water. If your tap TDS is low, some Drosera may tolerate it — test before committing an entire collection.

settings.question: Do all carnivorous plants need dormancy?
settings.answer: No. Temperate genera such as Sarracenia and Dionaea need a cool winter rest. Tropical Nepenthes and many Pinguicula grow year-round with stable warmth and humidity.

settings.question: Should I fertilise my plants?
settings.answer: Avoid conventional fertiliser — it burns roots. Let traps catch insects, or use very dilute foliar feeds only when you understand the genus.
